title: From Double-Click to Display: Map the Whole System
summary: Investigate one saved notice from input event to visible output and replace browser-only explanations with a complete systems model.
objectives: Assign input, operating-system, storage, browser, graphics, and display responsibilities.
Use an event trace to locate the boundary where a failed load stops.

steps.instruction: In the repair-cafe signal room, which account includes every required layer?

options.text: The HTML file wakes the monitor and directly instructs it which characters to illuminate.
Input reaches the operating system, which reads data for the browser to process before graphics and display hardware present pixels.
The browser performs input, storage access, graphics processing, and display output without operating-system or hardware services.

steps.title: Rebuild system chain · signal room
steps.instruction: Within the repair-cafe signal room, reconstruct the system chain in causal order.
steps.why: Separating system responsibilities makes a visible symptom traceable to the layer that can cause it. Ordering the repair-cafe signal room exposes its first unsupported causal handoff.

content.text: Do not order by vocabulary or visual position. Test whether each event creates the conditions required by the next event in the repair-cafe signal room.

hints: In the repair-cafe signal room sequence, locate the first event whose required input is not ready.
Separate repair-cafe signal room system chain events from later visible outcomes.
Rebuild the repair-cafe signal room sequence one causal handoff at a time.

options.text: Input hardware reports the open-file action
The operating system reads document bytes from storage
The browser parses the received bytes and prepares output
Graphics hardware and the display produce visible pixels

options.text: The browser permanently rewrote the file as soon as the pointer was clicked.
The operating system supplied stored document bytes before browser parsing began.
The display requested HTML tags directly from storage.

steps.title: Locate a failure · signal room
steps.instruction: In the repair-cafe signal room, the file bytes are read, but no browser process starts. Which boundary should be investigated next?
steps.why: Separating system responsibilities makes a visible symptom traceable to the layer that can cause it. Working through locate a failure narrows the repair-cafe signal room as you connect the explanation to evidence.

content.text: A web page is an outcome of cooperation. Input hardware reports an action; the operating system coordinates storage, memory, processes, and devices; the browser parses document data; graphics work prepares output; and the display emits pixels. The HTML file is data, not a self-running application. Apply this distinction during the repair-cafe signal room read task.

hints: During repair-cafe signal room read, identify which system chain boundary “Locate a failure” asks about.
Compare every repair-cafe signal room option with the named “Locate a failure” evidence during read.
For repair-cafe signal room read, reject claims that skip a required layer or invent unavailable evidence for “Locate a failure.”

options.text: The CSS color selected by the page author.
The operating-system handoff that launches or addresses the browser application.
The monitor brightness setting, because no parsing event exists yet.

options.text: HTML is executable machine code that runs without an application.
HTML is document data interpreted by a browser process running with operating-system and hardware support.
HTML is a physical display protocol sent directly to the monitor.

steps.title: Write the evidence note · signal room
steps.instruction: Write a concise case note for the repair-cafe signal room. Explain the mechanism, cite observed evidence, and reject one plausible alternative.
steps.why: Explaining the repair-cafe signal room from memory exposes gaps that selected answers can hide. Cite the mechanism and its evidence.

content.title: Transfer brief
content.text: A teammate must be able to reproduce your conclusion without seeing the earlier answer choices. Name evidence and limits, not only the final verdict.

hints: Start the repair-cafe signal room case note with the earliest observed event.
Use these repair-cafe signal room technical anchors where they fit: operating system, browser, display.
Close the repair-cafe signal room note by naming one alternative and the evidence that rules it out.
